title: lint: activate bashate
body:
- Activate bashate and resolves rule `E010`.
- Temporary disable other non-conforming rules, as they will be
  addressed in follow-ups
- Fixes python2 bug in embedded get_env_vars()
- Includes newline auto-fixed introduced by a previous merge

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *ssbarnea* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **tomsweeneyredhat** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @tomsweeneyredhat` in a comment when ready. LGTM
although not sure why images test failed.
@cevich @edsantiago PTAL

LGTM
The images tests seem to be failing everywhere in Libpod.  I'm not sure what's what there.
looking...

...Most of the changes here are purely cosmetic/style related but I can live with them.  Want to get @edsantiago to confirm on `test/system/helpers.bash`.  I think he had a workaround of sorts for this finding elsewhere, but I can't remember what it was.  Otherwise if we could just ignore that file, it could be an option as well.
Otherwise LGTM (after Ed peeks)

We've been through this before. In your first PR I mentioned that if this change is absolutely necessary it should (1) include a command-line option that prevents invoking this as a script, and (2) a good comment explaining that this is not a user-runnable script and describing the reason for the shebang (i.e. lint). Please see your previous PR for details.

> We've been through this before. In your first PR I mentioned that if this change is absolutely necessary it should (1) include a command-line option that prevents invoking this as a script, and (2) a good comment explaining that this is not a user-runnable script and describing the reason for the shebang (i.e. lint). Please see your previous PR for details.

Did you look the proposed shebang for the file? I think it documents very clear that is not a bash script. Look again. It is a bats file, let it be known.

Bashate will and does not run scripts, bit it looks for them. Shebang line is what any executable script should have, regardless which interpreter they use.
@ssbarnea Please rebase.  I believe this needs a rebase to get the images test to pass.

> It is unfortunate that we have to pollute our code by working around it.

Yeah, this is a trade-off vs maintaining an exception list for the linter, or using a different linter.  Arguably the shebang is/will be slightly easier to maintain long-term (by doing nothing), than the list (possibly updating it if the dir/file moves), or changing to a less-popular linter .  So...the least worst evil among a pile of dead suspected-witches :confounded:

> #!/usr/bin/env -S bash -c "echo This is not an actual bash script;exit 1"

```
^-- SC1008: This shebang was unrecognized. ShellCheck only supports sh/bash/dash/ksh. Add a 'shell' directive to specify.
^-- SC2096: On most OS, shebangs can only specify a single parameter.
```
I am I am afraid that SC2096 is quite a valid remark.
For fixing the CI, i raised https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5022

> ^-- SC1008: This shebang was unrecognized. ShellCheck only supports sh/bash/dash/ksh.
> ^-- SC2096: On most OS, shebangs can only specify a single parameter.

Fair enough; it was an ugly hack. I guess if the linter wants a shebang -- and this is a linter bug, not a BATS bug -- then let's give it a shebang. `#!/bin/bash` is fine. But please, I implore, leave the two-line comment (or equivalent) near the top. It might save someone time in the future, if they're not familiar with BATS.

As for why it's a linter bug (this is low priority, but just an FYI): there is a longstanding convention of storing library functions in a non-executable file with a `.sh` extension. BATS extends this convention to `.bash`, so a `load foo` statement in a `.bats` test will translate to `source foo.bash`. Perhaps it was an imperfect decision on the part of the BATS creators, perhaps they could have used `.bats-library` or `.potrzebie` instead, but they chose `.bash` as something meaningful, that stands out as different in a directory full of `.bats` files; and at this point that is unlikely to change. I believe that a linter should understand that a non-executable file should not have a shebang. 

Thanks again for your perseverance. I realize there are a lot of details to consider; our common goal is to spend time early to get those details right, so we don't have to spend (usually more) time in the future figuring out breakages.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4951) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@ssbarnea: PR needs rebase.

@ssbarnea Still working on this?
Friendly ping.
I am not working on this (lack of time), so anyone is welcomed to takeover this.
I think we can probably just close this for now then.  If someone wants to take up the helm, I'm happy to help as always, but I think we have 'bigger fish to fry' for the near-term.
